title: raukaua × serratus
intro: Raukaua × serratus, also known as the Kawakawa, is a plant species native to New Zealand, characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and woody stem. The medicinal properties of Raukaua × serratus are attributed to its ability to stimulate the nervous system and act as an antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant agent. The health benefits of this plant include relief from muscle spasms, digestive issues, and skin problems, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cognitive function. Raukaua × serratus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or ointment, making it a versatile herbal remedy.
